assertions.title: Connected wallet address used as sender

Provider menu displayed with Frame and Mock Wallet options

/compare request URL: ...&sender=0xDeAdBeEf00000000000000000000000000000001 — matches mock wallet address exactly
Page loaded with 'Connect Wallet' button, no wallet connected

steps.action: Select Ethereum chain, click Compare Quotes
steps.observed: /compare URL: chainId=1&from=...&to=...&amount=1000&slippageBps=50 — no sender= parameter at all

evidence.urlBar: Both with and without wallet: URL contains only chainId, from, to, amount, slippageBps. Sender never appears in URL.

steps.expected: Page loads normally, sender param ignored
steps.observed: Page loaded, form restored values (Ethereum, USDC, WETH, 1000, 50), auto-submitted comparison, results displayed successfully
Console: 0 errors. Sender param silently ignored.

evidence.consoleErrors: none — zero errors when loading URL with old sender= parameter
evidence.network: Page auto-submitted comparison and received 200 OK results

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. The sender input has been completely removed from the DOM and source code. The connected wallet address is correctly used as the sender parameter in /compare API requests. The sender parameter is never exposed in the URL bar. Auto-refresh correctly propagates the wallet address. Old bookmarked URLs with sender= are silently ignored with no JS errors.
